title: Fourier Ptychographic Microscopy (FPM)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical domain literature for this specific principle.

E.description: Fourier Ptychographic Microscopy (FPM): angular illumination scan produces the measurement through a 4-node primitive DAG S.scan.led_angle -> L.fourier_aperture -> L.modulus_squared -> int.angular, with multi-angle tomographic integration and photon-shot-noise-limited (Poisson counting). Recovery is posed as a non-convex inverse problem that inverts the forward operator to estimate the scene-side complex 2D. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~80; calibration-level mismatch (LED_position_error, LED_intensity_variation, partial_coherence) sets the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form imaging equation.
E.world_state_x: Scene parameter of interest (density / complex field / lifetime / normals depending on spec).
E.observation_y: Measured intensity or photon counts from the imaging system.

W.regime: Existence of the recovered complex 2D is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness is local rather than global (non-convex landscape); convergence depends on initialisation and priors. Stability is ill-conditioned (kappa_eff ~= 80); LED_position_error dominates the stability cliff; LED_intensity_variation and the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Photon-shot-noise-limited (poisson counting) sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor, while TV / wavelet-sparsity / deep priors stabilise recovery at the ill-conditioned end of Omega.

C.solver_class: iterative projection (ADMM / GAP) + optimisation [GS-FPM, EPIE-FPM] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [DeepFPM]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sqrt(sigma_signal^2) / sqrt(photon_count). Mismatch-induced bias adds + C2 * |LED_position_error| + C3 * ||x - x_s||_1 (best s-sparse / low-rank approximation residual). Iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the MLE; epsilon_fn captures the monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * log(...)) per iteration; learned variants: O(H W Z * F_theta_cost) per forward pass
